Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single artisanal luxury fashion item available for sale in the Versare e-commerce store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product, including its design inspiration, craftsmanship, and aesthetic.

price.description: The current selling price of the product.

materials.description: A list of primary materials used in the product, reflecting natural and luxurious fabrics.

mainImageUrl.description: The URL for the primary editorial image of the product.

additionalImageUrls.description: A list of URLs for additional images of the product, showcasing different angles or details.

availableSizes.description: A list of available sizes for the product (e.g., 'S', 'M', 'L', 'One Size').

availableColors.description: A list of available colors for the product.

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for inventory management.

stockQuantity.description: The current number of units available in stock for this product.

collectionId.description: Reference to the Collection this product belongs to. (Relationship: Collection 1:N Product)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was added to the catalog.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the product details.

Collection.title: Collection
Collection.description: Groups related products under a specific theme or seasonal release, like 'Brasil-core' or 'Classic Europe'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Collection entity.

name.description: The name of the collection (e.g., 'Summer Solstice', 'Brasil Core').

description.description: A description of the collection's theme and inspiration.

heroImageUrl.description: The URL for a hero image representing the collection, used on collection pages.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the collection was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the collection details.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores non-authentication related user data, enabling personalized experiences like cart management and AI recommendations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically linked to an external authentication system's user ID.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

defaultShippingAddressId.description: Reference to the user's preferred shipping address. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

defaultBillingAddressId.description: Reference to the user's preferred billing address. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

browsingHistoryProductIds.description: A list of product IDs the user has recently viewed, used for AI recommendations. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Product)

wishlistProductIds.description: A list of product IDs the user has added to their wishlist. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Product)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the user profile details.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Stores physical address details associated with a user, used for shipping and billing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this address belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

street2.description: Optional secondary address information (e.g., apartment number, suite).

state.description: The state or province of the address.

postalCode.description: The postal code of the address.

isShipping.description: Indicates if this address can be used for shipping.

isBilling.description: Indicates if this address can be used for billing.

label.description: An optional label for the address (e.g., 'Home', 'Work').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the address was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the address details.

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a user's active shopping cart, holding items selected for purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cart entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this cart. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Cart - active cart)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last modification to the cart (e.g., item added/removed, quantity changed).

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a single product entry within a user's shopping cart, including selected options and quantity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

cartId.description: Reference to the Cart this item belongs to. (Relationship: Cart 1:N CartItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product added to the cart. (Relationship: Product 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product in the cart.

selectedSize.description: The size chosen for the product.

selectedColor.description: The color chosen for the product.

priceAtAddToCart.description: The price of the product at the moment it was added to the cart, for historical accuracy.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the item was added to the cart.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to this cart item (e.g., quantity change).

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Records a completed transaction for a user, detailing the purchase summary.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed the order. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total amount paid for the order, including shipping and tax.

shippingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address used for shipping this order. (Relationship: Order 1:N Address)

billingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address used for billing this order. (Relationship: Order 1:N Address)

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Shipped', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

paymentMethod.description: The payment method used for the order (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'PayPal', 'Pix').

trackingNumber.description: The tracking number for the shipment, if available.

shippingCost.description: The cost of shipping for this order.

taxAmount.description: The total tax amount applied to this order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the order status or details.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Details a specific product included in an order, preserving its state at the time of purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product purchased. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product purchased in this order item.

unitPriceAtOrder.description: The unit price of the product at the exact time the order was placed, for accurate historical records.

subtotal.description: The subtotal for this order item (quantity * unitPriceAtOrder).

selectedSize.description: The size of the product selected at the time of order.

selectedColor.description: The color of the product selected at the time of order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the order item details.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all artisanal luxury fashion collections. Publicly readable by all users, ensuring discoverability. No specific authorization fields needed within documents as access is global read.

Top-level collection for all individual luxury fashion products. Publicly readable by all users for browsing and product detail viewing. No specific authorization fields needed within documents as access is global read.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Root collection for all user-specific data. Each document represents a user's profile and is owned by the user themselves. Access is restricted to the authenticated user whose UID matches the 'userId' path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Subcollection under a user's profile for storing their shipping and billing addresses. Each address document includes the 'userId' field, explicitly linking it to the owner for direct authorization checks, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this address.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific address.

definition.description: Subcollection under a user's profile to manage their active shopping cart. Each cart document includes the 'userId' field, explicitly linking it to the owner for direct authorization checks, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this cart.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user's shopping cart.

definition.description: Subcollection within a specific user's cart, storing individual product items selected for purchase. For Authorization Independence, each CartItem document MUST include a denormalized 'userId' field (in addition to 'cartId') to enable direct ownership checks without querying the parent cart.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent cart.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent cart.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific item within the cart.

definition.description: Subcollection under a user's profile for their completed orders. Each order document includes the 'userId' field, explicitly linking it to the owner for direct authorization checks, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed this order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific order.

definition.description: Subcollection within a specific user's order, detailing individual products purchased. For Authorization Independence, each OrderItem document MUST include a denormalized 'userId' field (in addition to 'orderId') to enable direct ownership checks without querying the parent order.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific item within the order.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. Publicly viewable entities, such as 'Collections' and 'Products', are placed in top-level collections (`/collections` and `/products`). This allows for simple, wide-open read access rules without any authorization checks, fulfilling the QAP for public data display.

All user-specific and private data (`UserProfile`, `Address`, `Cart`, `CartItem`, `Order`, `OrderItem`) are structured under `/users/{userId}/...` paths. This path-based ownership is highly secure and simplifies authorization rules significantly: a user can only access data where `{userId}` matches their `request.auth.uid`. This immediately satisfies QAPs for private data, ensuring that `list` operations (e.g., listing all addresses for a user) automatically filter results based on the authenticated user.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) and Denormalization:**
To ensure authorization independence and eliminate the need for `get()` operations in security rules, crucial authorization context is denormalized directly into relevant subcollection documents. Specifically:

*   **Addresses:** The `Address` entity already includes `userId` in its schema. When stored at `/users/{userId}/addresses/{addressId}`, the `userId` in the document directly matches the path's wildcard, enabling atomic and independent authorization checks.
*   **Carts:** The `Cart` entity already includes `userId` in its schema. When stored at `/users/{userId}/carts/{cartId}`, similar to addresses, the `userId` facilitates direct authorization.
*   **CartItems:** A `CartItem` depends on its parent `Cart` for ownership. To make it authorization-independent, the `userId` (owner of the cart) MUST be denormalized and included in every `CartItem` document. This allows rules on `/users/{userId}/carts/{cartId}/items/{cartItemId}` to directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without needing to fetch the parent `Cart` document.
*   **Orders:** The `Order` entity already includes `userId` in its schema. When stored at `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`, the `userId` facilitates direct authorization.
*   **OrderItems:** Similar to `CartItems`, an `OrderItem` depends on its parent `Order` for ownership. The `userId` (owner of the order) MUST be denormalized and included in every `OrderItem` document. This enables direct authorization checks on `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}/items/{orderItemId}` against `resource.data.userId`.

This denormalization strategy eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, supports atomic operations, and makes security rules robust and easy to debug. Structural segregation (public vs. private data) further enhances rule simplicity, as each collection or subcollection generally has a homogeneous security posture.
